english: II. vb., pf. bsres (bsres), fut. bsre (bsre), imp. (b)sres ((b)sres), trans. to °dré-ba ('dre ba) 1. to mix with, to mingle, to admix, már-la sré-ba (mar la sre ba) to mix with butter Lt., čaṅ ču sre-ba (chang chu sre ba) to mix beer with water Med.; dreu sré-ba (dreu sre ba) to breed mules; bsrés-pa (bsres pa) mixed up, confused, of a narration Tar.; fig. ka (kha) or lus sré-ba (lus sre ba) to communicate with another, i.e. to live, to eat, drink, smoke with a person Do.; skyid sdug sré-ba (skyid sdug sre ba) to share pleasure and pain, joy and sorrow Glr.; W., like *żé-če* (*zhe ce*), to exchange for: *zan daṅ šróg* (*zan dang shrog*) to risk one's life for a subsistence. - 2. to add; to add up, cast up, sum up Wdk., *nyī daṅ nyī šre żi* (*nyi dang nyi shre zhi*) 2 and 2 make 4 W.
weasel, prob. = *la-kyi-mo* (*la kyi mo*) W.; sré-mo (sre mo) Lex.
1. Sch.: the sinew above the heel. - 2. n. of a medicine?

I. sbst. (W. *šrag-pa* (*shrag pa*)) partridge. II. vb., pf. (b)sregs ((b)sregs), fut. bsreg (bsreg), imp. (b)sreg(s) ((b)sreg(s)), W. *šrág-če* (*shrag ce*) to burn, i.e. 1. to consume, to destroy by or with fire (mes, mé-la (mes, me la)) e.g. a corpse, dág-mo (dag mo), or W. *nán-tan* (*nan tan*), altogether, entirely, dgra (dgra) an enemy (sc. in effigy); sbyín-sreg (sbyin sreg) burnt-offering; to make red-hot, lčags-bsrégs (lcags bsregs) red-hot iron Thgy. - 2. to roast, fry, bake, on a spit C., or in a pan, *már-la* (*mar la*) in butter W.; *tá-gir šrág-če* (*ta gir shrag ce*) to bake bread W.; to tan, to make swarthy, nyí-mas (nyi mas) (to be tanned) by the sun Dzl.; bsrég-kaṅ (bsreg khang) Sch. shed for storing up fire-wood.
mi-sréṅ (mi sreng) C. = mi-rkyáṅ (mi rkyang), v. rkyaṅ-pa (rkyang pa).
a deity of the Bonpo Mil.

1. vb., sbst., adj. to desire, the desire, desirous, zás-la (zas la) of food Lt., ka-tsai ró-la (kha tsai ro la) of acid or hot substances Med., ról-mo-la (rol mo la) (liking) music Stg.; yúl-sred-pa čúṅ-ba (yul sred pa chung ba) not much attached to his native country; °jig-rtén(-la) sréd-pa ('jig rten(-la) sred pa) avarice, covetousness Mil., °dod-sred-čan ('dod sred can) covetous, greedy Pth., čágs-sred-čan (chags sred can) lecherous, libidinous Pth.; sréd-pa-las yóṅs-su gról-ba (sred pa las yongs su grol ba) quite free of any desire, (so is Buddha); sréd-po (sred po) Cs. lover, sréd-ma (sred ma) Cs. sweet-heart. - 2. symb. num.: 8.
pf. and fut. bsrel (bsrel) Cs., W. *šrál če* (*shral ce*) to bring up, to rear, to nurse up, to train, infants, young animals, *šrál-kan* (*shral kan*) nourisher, fosterer, nurse etc.

resp. tugs-sro (thugs sro). W., heat, ardour, passion, wrath, anger, *šro yoṅ* (*shro yong*) anger rises (in a person), he (etc.) grows angry, *šro bab* (*shro bab*), šro bud* (*shro bud*) the anger abates; *šro-ríṅ-mo* (*shro ring mo*) slow to wrath, *šró-čan* (*shro can*) furious, raging, *šro-túṅ* (*shro thung*) hot, ardent, passionate.

pf. (b)sros ((b)sros), fut. bsro (bsro), imp. (b)sro(s) ((b)sro(s)), to warm, to make warm or hot at the fire, or in the sun Glr., Lt.; °jám-pai dród-kyis bu bsro ('jam pai drod kyis bu bsro) (a mother) foments her child with a gentle warmth Thgy.

1. egg of a louse, a nit C., W., šig-sro °du (shig sro 'du) nits are increasing fast S.g. - 2. small bubble W., *čáṅ-la šró-ma kol* (*chang la shro ma khol*) the beer foams, froths in fermentation. - 3. sró-ma nág-po (sro ma nag po), sró-ma séṅ-ge (sro ma seng ge) n. of a medicinal herb Med.

life, srog gčód-pa (srog gcod pa) to kill, frq.; srog lén-pa (srog len pa), °próg-pa ('phrog pa) id., esp. when done by demons; srog daṅ °prál-ba (srog dang 'phral ba) id., esp. to execute, to put to death Glr., srog daṅ °brál-ba (srog dang 'bral ba) to die; srog °búl-ba (srog 'bul ba) Dzl. 2, 12 Sch.: to sacrifice, to yield up one's life, but the manuscript of Kyelang has: srog daṅ °brál-lo (srog dang 'bral lo), and šá-bai srog kyéd-la °búl-lo (sha bai srog khyed la 'bul lo) (Mil.) means: I make you a present of the stag's life, i.e. I spare its life for your sake; srog °dór-ba (srog 'dor ba) to sacrifice, one's life, prop. to cast it away Dzl.; srog-la mi ltá-ba (srog la mi lta ba) to make light of one's life frq.; srog daṅ bsdó-ba (srog dang bsdo ba) to risk, to hazard one's life, frq.; srog skyób-pa (srog skyob pa) to save life Dzl., srog byín-pa (srog byin pa), °don-pa ('don pa) id., Thgy.: to save, to preserve (a child's) life (by well caring for it); srog °tsó-ba (srog 'tsho ba) id. Dzl., S.g. (Sch. also: to recover, to grow
